title: Old Dinosaur Footprints Found on Beach
summary: Scientists found old dinosaur footprints on a beach in South Africa. The footprints are 132 million years old! That is a very long time ago. The footprints were found on rocks near the water. Some footprints are flat on the rock, and some are inside the cliffs. This is special because there are not many dinosaur footprints from that time in southern Africa. The place where they found the footprints is small, only as long as a swimming pool. But finding many footprints there means lots of dinosaurs lived in that area long ago. These are the youngest dinosaur footprints ever found in southern Africa. They help scientists learn more about dinosaurs that lived after big volcanic eruptions. The footprints were made by a kind of dinosaur called a theropod, which walked on two legs. This discovery is very exciting for scientists who study dinosaurs.

background_read: Dinosaurs lived on Earth millions of years ago. They came in many shapes and sizes. Some walked on two legs, like the theropod that made these footprints. Scientists study fossils, like footprints and bones, to learn about dinosaurs. Footprints can tell us how dinosaurs moved and where they lived. The footprints in this story are very old and help us understand dinosaurs that lived after big volcanic eruptions.
Article_Structure: WHAT: Dinosaur footprints that are 132 million years old.
WHERE: On a beach in South Africa.
WHY: To learn about dinosaurs that lived long ago.

perspectives.description: They are excited because the footprints are rare and help them learn about dinosaurs in southern Africa.
They might be amazed to see such old footprints on a beach.
